document.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Merative ™ Creating external applications with the Cúram Web Design System

root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Merative ™ Creating external applications with the Cúram Web Design System
root.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 guide “Creating external applications with the Cúram Web Design System,” covering pages 1–12. It includes the title/edition pages, a note directing readers to the legal notices, and the table of contents. The main substantive content is Chapter 1, which introduces the Cúram Web Design System as a React-based framework for building responsive, accessible external web apps with REST API integration and theming. It explains three persona paths: citizen benefit applications, authorized representative/supporter applications, and non-benefits apps built from scratch. It also notes that citizen/representative solutions depend on Cúram Platform plus Universal Access and the Web Design System, while other external apps require only the Platform and the Web Design System. The remaining pages contain legal notices: usage permissions, export and redistribution restrictions, “AS-IS” warranty disclaimers, patent and third-party product notices, privacy policy, trademarks, and sample code license terms.

children.title: Note
children.summary: This note tells readers to review the Notices section on page 11 before using the document or the product it describes. It also includes the copyright notice for Merative US L.P. (2012, 2024).

children.title: Edition
children.summary: Edition and document front matter for Cúram 8.1.2, including copyright/trademark notices and the table of contents. Also introduces Chapter 1 on the Cúram Web Design System: what it is, supported personas (citizens, authorized representatives, other users), sample/reference applications, use of REST APIs, theming, and platform dependencies.

children.title: Notices
Privacy policy
children.summary: This section covers legal and publishing notices: fictitious names in examples, sample code licensing terms, warranty/liability disclaimers, the Merative privacy policy link, and trademark attributions for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft, and UNIX. It also notes copyright ownership by Merative US L.P. (2012, 2024).

children.title: Trademarks
children.summary: Lists trademark and copyright notices for Merative and third-party brands used in the document. States that example names are fictitious, sample programs may be copied and modified under the Merative Client Agreement, and are provided “AS IS” without warranty. Includes the privacy policy link. Identifies trademarks for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft, and UNIX, plus a note that other names may be trademarks of others.

Legal and usage notices for the publication: permissions for personal and enterprise use, restrictions on redistribution/derivative works, export-law compliance, and Merative’s right to revoke permissions. Includes strong “AS-IS” warranty/liability disclaimers, patent notice, non-Merative product and website disclaimers, and copyright © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: Table of contents for the Cúram 8.1.2 document. Lists front matter entries such as Note and Edition, then points to Chapter 1, “Creating external applications with the Cúram Web Design System,” starting on page 9. Also includes notices and legal items like Privacy policy and Trademarks, with copyright information. Useful for locating major sections rather than substantive content.

children.title: 1 Creating external applications with the Cúram Web Design System
children.summary: Introduces the Cúram Web Design System as a React-based framework for building responsive external web applications with accessible UI, authentication for external users, Cúram Web API integration, and theming support. Explains how to choose a persona: citizen apps, apps for authorized representatives or similar external actors, or non-benefits apps built from scratch. Notes that citizen and representative solutions depend on Cúram Platform plus Universal Access and the Web Design System, while other apps need only the Platform and Web Design System. Includes sample/reference application pointers and standard legal notices.
